episode_meta.title: Florent Daudens: Building an AI-Literate Newsroom at Radio Canada

guests.name: Florent Daudens
guests.role: Director of News Gathering and Deployment

use_cases.description: Three-hour AI training sessions for journalists covering AI fundamentals, ethics, and prompt engineering, training approximately 100 people in three months
use_cases.outcome: Shift in journalist attitudes from fear/replacement to creativity/possibility; highest scores for concrete applicability among all training sessions

use_cases.description: Cross-functional AI expertise group meeting every two to three weeks to brainstorm coverage topics, share developments, and discuss ongoing projects, with a Google Group for async exchange
use_cases.outcome: Better understanding of AI issues and generation of news story ideas

use_cases.description: Internal prompt library hosted on Google Sheets where journalists contribute and gradually improve shared prompts
use_cases.outcome: Pooling of prompts as a common organizational tool rather than individual artisanal use

use_cases.description: Prompt clinic as a level-two follow-up workshop where journalists break down their job tasks and identify which ones AI could assist with, producing concrete tools (prompts, programs, Google Colabs, GPTs)
use_cases.outcome: Opportunity identification exercise producing concrete, short-iteration tools

use_cases.description: Using ChatGPT vision to extract text from scanned access-to-information request documents and generate Excel files
use_cases.outcome: Significant time saver for extracting data from scanned documents that could not otherwise be machine-read

use_cases.description: Generating wrong answers for news quizzes, leveraging the model's tendency to generate correct answers by prompting it for incorrect ones
use_cases.outcome: Creative and funny wrong answers for quiz content

use_cases.description: Twitter/X thread generation tool that takes an article and generates a tweet or thread with a specified number of tweets in a chosen language for a specific audience
use_cases.outcome: Significant time saver for content adaptation; synthesis exercise works well though always needs tailoring

use_cases.description: Hallmark movie scenario generator created for Christmas using AI
Probing election results database using natural language queries
use_cases.outcome: Impressive quality of responses though prompt costs could be high; opened to LinkedIn community for stress testing and iteration

use_cases.description: Content adaptation from TV story text to web article drafts, and from TV stories to TikTok scripts using few-shot prompts or fine-tuning
use_cases.outcome: Significant potential time gain for journalists managing multiple platforms (TV, radio, web, TikTok, news apps)

use_cases.description: Transcription of all radio and TV content produced, unlocking data for indexing, entity recognition, internal training, and accessibility
use_cases.outcome: Archives department already seeing their work evolve; potential to unlock a torrent of data from electronic media heritage

use_cases.description: Automatic fact-checking of numbers said by politicians on air against statistical institute databases
use_cases.outcome: Automatic verification when a politician states a number on-air

use_cases.description: RAG-based AI teaching assistant for university digital journalism course, ingesting course notes and transcribed class recordings
use_cases.outcome: Students can clarify concepts, get feedback on assignments; teacher can log questions to identify gaps in instruction; described as most important pedagogical change in eight years of teaching

use_cases.description: Using ChatGPT to help students learn data visualization coding (D3.js) by interacting with the model to generate code and understand syntax
use_cases.outcome: Students can interact with machines more naturally to produce code outputs and learn fundamentals

use_cases.description: Brainstorming course plan for digital journalism course using ChatGPT
use_cases.outcome: Generated an eight-point plan; not used as-is but prompted deeper reflection on existing course plan

use_cases.description: Custom GPT with ingested Mandarin textbook for personal language learning practice
use_cases.outcome: Effective practice tool for learning Mandarin

use_cases.description: AI-cloned avatar speaking Mandarin Chinese using HeyGen
use_cases.outcome: Teacher said the AI clone spoke better Chinese than Daudens himself

use_cases.description: Automated daily AI newsletter synthesizing approximately 50 sources including research papers, hosted on AWS with a cron job
use_cases.outcome: Daily inbox summary of most important AI news and research papers; built by interacting with ChatGPT to learn AWS and cron scheduling

use_cases.description: Using Google Pinpoint to ingest large document collections (up to 200,000 documents) including videos, audio, images for investigation and research purposes
use_cases.organization: Radio Canada (mentioned as general journalism tool)
use_cases.outcome: Automatic tagging, indexing, transcription, text extraction from images; efficient for investigation purposes

key_claims.claim: Only 10% of newsrooms train their journalists in AI, and 13% of newsrooms don't even consider training their staff in AI
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: In three months, approximately 100 people at Radio Canada received AI training
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: At the beginning of training sessions, journalists associate AI with words like fear, replacement, loss of control, and octopus; by the end, they use words like creativity, sense of control, and possibility
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: The training session scores among the highest for concrete applicability of learning to work
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: GPT-4 scores approximately 85% in English and 83% in French on a three-shot accuracy benchmark on MMLU
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: Mixtral 8x7B scores slightly better on the MMLU benchmark in French than in English
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: An LLM is not a search engine and should not be used as one
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: Hallucinations are deeply embedded in the concept of AI and are in a way necessary
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: With Radio Canada and CBC combined, they are probably the place where the most feeds, shootings, and live broadcasts converge in the news media in Canada every day
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: Google Pinpoint allows uploading up to 200,000 documents per collection
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

key_claims.claim: The Andrew Ng machine learning specialization is approximately 100 hours
key_claims.speaker: Florent Daudens

challenges.description: Insufficient time and training budget to train everyone in the newsroom; AI training is not Daudens' full-time job
French language models were historically poor; trained on French from France, not Quebec French, leading to high error rates with Quebec accents
Cultural sovereignty issues with AI models — e.g., Mistral's first model (7B) was trained only in English despite being a French startup
If journalists miss exercising editorial judgment and rely on AI summarization, they risk missing the lead of important stories (e.g., the Prime Minister press conference example)
Individual use of ChatGPT remains artisanal and not very effective; value is only created by pooling prompts and sharing collectively
Newsrooms do not naturally think of themselves as tech companies; collaboration with developers is not natural for journalists
Personalization of news raises tension between informing the general public with diverse perspectives versus serving individual user preferences
Cost of prompts can be high when querying structured data like election results databases
Resources are increasingly limited while the number of platforms to fill (TV, radio, TikTok, web, apps) keeps growing
Risk of overloading developers who don't have time to work on AI projects; need for short concrete iterations instead

quotes.text: If we don't know what we're talking about, if we don't know the possibilities, we will just miss the train and then realize that we have to use it but we don't know how and it's gonna be scary
quotes.speaker: Florent Daudens

quotes.text: Sometimes you can't rely on summarization for an important news conference from the Prime Minister if you haven't heard it. If you haven't exercised your editorial judgment to know what was important for this press conference.
quotes.speaker: Florent Daudens
quotes.topic: Limits of AI summarization vs. editorial judgment

quotes.text: If we each use ChatGPT in a corner it will remain something artisanal and not very effective
quotes.speaker: Florent Daudens

quotes.text: We need to think ourselves as both content and tech companies. It's crucial, I think.
quotes.speaker: Florent Daudens

quotes.text: I think above all it's curiosity. Because a machine will not spontaneously ask itself a question. And the other thing is editorial judgment.
quotes.speaker: Florent Daudens
quotes.topic: Human singularity in journalism vs. AI

quotes.text: The best use cases, and I'm convinced about that, will come from people using it in their operations.
quotes.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Train journalists in AI basics including what AI is and is not, prompt engineering, and ethical guidelines; demystify AI so people feel empowered rather than scared
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Build cross-functional expertise groups including journalists, developers, analysts, and people from other departments to share AI knowledge
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Create a shared internal prompt library rather than having individuals use AI tools in isolation; conceive prompts as a common organizational tool
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Encourage journalists to make mistakes with AI because mistakes are the starting point for learning
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Do not use LLMs as search engines; instead ground them to databases or web search tools when factual retrieval is needed
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: When editorial judgment is required, do not rely on AI; only use AI for tasks where editorial judgment is not at stake
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Think about AI implementation using specialized tools or models rather than general public tools like ChatGPT 3.5
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Break down job tasks and identify which ones AI could assist with, rather than approaching AI from a general employment angle
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Newsrooms should think of themselves as both content businesses and tech companies
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Rethink how information is presented to the public vs. what is sent to external platforms like search engines or ChatGPT; consider new forms of journalism beyond classical web articles
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

recommendations.advice: Take Andrew Ng's courses (free short courses and machine learning specialization) to gain theoretical and practical foundations in AI
recommendations.speaker: Florent Daudens

external_references: Reuters Institute study on media training for AI (finding: only 10% of newsrooms train journalists in AI)
Andrew Ng's courses at Stanford University (short courses and machine learning specialization)
Gallicagram dataset (French newspapers, 85 billion words, for fine-tuning models in French)
Jeff Jarvis (referenced prior Newsroom Robots episode)
Philippe Bhatt / Philippe Baudouin (AI entrepreneur, Montreal)
Radio France (research institute project on fact-checking politician statements)
Amazon (early Quebec French speech model training)
University of Montreal (where Daudens teaches digital journalism)
